assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and is recognised as an intragroup monetary receivable/payable.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation because the group entities involved have different functional currencies.
The normal IFRS 9 hedge designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are satisfied.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable can be treated as a recognised monetary asset whose foreign exchange risk component is designated in a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also contemplates cash flow hedging of a particular risk on a recognised asset or liability, which can be relevant to the foreign-currency cash flow variability of the receivable.
